columns.name: employee_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each employee, sourced from MySQL EMPLOYEE_ID primary key.

columns.name: phone_number
columns.description: Employee phone number; nullable as not all employees have a number on record.

columns.name: hire_date
columns.description: Date the employee was hired; used as the partition column to support date-range analytics and efficient pruning.

columns.name: job_id
columns.description: Identifier for the employee job role; used as a cluster column.

columns.name: salary
columns.description: Employee base salary with up to two decimal places of precision.

columns.name: commission_pct
columns.description: Commission percentage applicable to eligible employees; nullable.

columns.name: manager_id
columns.description: Self-referential FK referencing employee_id of the employee manager; nullable for top-level employees.

columns.name: department_id
columns.description: Identifier for the department the employee belongs to; used as a cluster column.

columns.name: status
columns.description: Employment status value such as active or inactive; used as a cluster column.

columns.name: created_date
columns.description: Timestamp when the employee record was created in the source system.

columns.name: updated_date
columns.description: Timestamp of the last update to the employee record in the source; serves as the high-watermark for daily incremental loads.

cardinality.notes: Many employees may report to one manager via manager_id referencing employee_id within the same table. Top-level employees have a NULL manager_id.
